title: vatica lewisiana
intro: Vatica lewisiana, also known as the Borneo ironwood, is a tree species native to Borneo that belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae. The bark and leaves of the Vatica lewisiana plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Vatica lewisiana include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Vatica lewisiana is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and its extracts are also used in the production of traditional herbal remedies.
